scenarios.scenarioName: Dentist appointment creates event, not task

assertionResults.description: An event should be created for the appointment
A task should NOT be created for an appointment
assertionResults.message: No unwanted task matching "dentist" was created

scenarios.responseText:
Noted and logged, sir. Agent has created a **Dentist Appointment** event for **Tuesday, April 28 at 2:00 PM** (one hour). You'll find it waiting in your vault, ready and punctual as any good appointment should be.

Might Agent suggest adding a reminder task the day before, or noting the location of the practice?

scenarios.scenarioName: Buying groceries creates task, not event

assertionResults.description: A task should be created for buying groceries
An event should NOT be created for a chore
assertionResults.message: No unwanted event matching "grocer" was created

scenarios.responseText:
Consider it done, sir. Agent has created a task — **Buy groceries** — due this **Saturday, April 25**. It's sitting in your to-do list at medium priority, ready when you are.

Should you wish to add a shopping list or link this to a specific store or goal, Agent is most happy to assist.

scenarios.scenarioName: Marathon goal creates goal, not task

assertionResults.description: A goal should be created for a long-term objective
A task should NOT be created for a yearly goal
assertionResults.message: No unwanted task matching "marathon" was created

scenarios.responseText:
Splendid ambition, sir! Agent has created the goal **Run a Marathon** — marked as high priority, active, with a target date of **December 31, 2026**. Plenty of time to build up those miles properly.

A few suggestions to set you up for success:
- **Break it into milestones** — a 5K, 10K, and half-marathon along the way
- **Create a training plan task** — Agent can track weekly targets if you'd like
- **Log a race** — if you have a specific marathon in mind, Agent can add it as an event

Shall Agent sketch out some initial training milestones?

scenarios.scenarioName: Wifi password creates note, not task

assertionResults.description: A note should be created for a piece of info to remember
A task should NOT be created for remembering info
assertionResults.message: No unwanted task matching "wifi" was created

scenarios.responseText:
Noted and safely tucked away, sir. Agent has created a note titled **WiFi Password** containing the credentials `sunflower42`, tagged with `wifi`, `password`, and `credentials` for easy retrieval whenever you need it.

Simply ask "What's the wifi password?" and Agent shall have it at the ready.

scenarios.scenarioName: Marking a task done updates it, does not create a new one

assertionResults.description: Existing task should be updated
Task status should be set to complete
Should still have exactly 1 task (no duplicate created)

scenarios.responseText: Consider it done, sir. **Buy groceries** has been marked as complete. Is there anything else on the list Agent can help you strike off today?

scenarios.scenarioName: Adding a new task alongside an existing one

assertionResults.description: A new task should be created for cleaning the kitchen
Should have 2 tasks total (existing + new)

scenarios.responseText: Consider it noted, sir. Agent has created the task **Clean the kitchen** — status open, priority medium. It awaits your attention whenever you're ready. Shall Agent set a due date or priority adjustment for it?

scenarios.scenarioName: Create both a task and a goal in one request

assertionResults.description: A task should be created for fixing the fence
A goal should be created for improving the backyard

scenarios.responseText:
Right away, sir — and all done! Agent has created the following:

- ✅ **Task:** Fix the fence *(medium priority, open)*
- 🎯 **Goal:** Improve the backyard *(medium priority, active)*

Agent has also taken the liberty of linking **Fix the fence** → **Improve the backyard**, as the two are clearly kindred endeavours. Should you wish to add a due date to the task or a target date for the goal, simply say the word.

scenarios.scenarioName: Stop-doing statement creates a todont

assertionResults.description: A todont should be created for the habit to stop
A task should NOT be created for a todont
assertionResults.message: No unwanted task matching "phone" was created

scenarios.responseText:
Noted and filed, sir. Agent has created a **todont** — *Check phone before bed* — with the reason: phone use before bed disrupts sleep quality and undermines a proper wind-down routine.

It sits in the vault as a standing commitment to yourself, not a task to tick off, but a habit to honour.

Should Agent link this to a broader goal — something like sleep quality or a wellness ambition — if one exists or if you'd like to create one?

scenarios.scenarioName: Adding a contact creates a person entity with fields
Phaibel can identify the model it is using
Soccer parent creates a practice schedule

assertionResults.description: Response should mention Emma
Response should mention soccer

scenarios.responseText:
Agent must confess to a small hiccup, sir — the "people" entity type does not appear to be configured in the vault just yet, which prevented Emma from being saved as a contact and the links from being established.

However, the recurring event itself was created successfully:

- 📅 **Soccer Practice (Emma)** — every **Tuesday at 5:00 PM** at **Northfield Park**

To fully resolve this, Agent would recommend running `phaibel entity-types add person` (or checking `~/.phaibel/entity-types.json`) to enable the *person* type, after which Agent can create an entry for **Emma** (daughter) and link her to the practice schedule.

Shall Agent attempt that again once the entity type is in place?

scenarios.scenarioName: Car enthusiast adds a vehicle to their collection

assertionResults.description: Response should mention the Mustang

scenarios.responseText:
Agent must confess to a minor stumble, sir — a validation hiccup caused the vehicle record to fail saving. The system expected numeric values for *year* and *mileage*, but they arrived as text strings. The entity was not persisted, I'm afraid.

Agent will resolve this promptly. Your **2019 Ford Mustang GT** (VIN: `1FA6P8CF7K5410123`, 42,000 miles) is worth remembering properly, and Agent has flagged this for a corrected save. In the meantime, the details are noted here and will be captured via the assumed node below.

Apologies for the inconvenience — Agent shall have this sorted posthaste.

scenarios.scenarioName: Football coach adds players to the roster

assertionResults.description: Response should mention Jake
Response should mention Marcus

scenarios.responseText:
Two new players have been added to the roster, sir — and Agent took the liberty of establishing a **Player** entity type to keep things properly organised going forward.

**Added to the roster:**
- 🏈 **Jake Williams** — #12, Quarterback
- 🏈 **Marcus Johnson** — #44, Linebacker

Both are filed under `players/` and tagged as Active by default. Should you wish to link them to a specific team entity or track stats, Agent is at the ready.

scenarios.scenarioName: Home cook saves a recipe

assertionResults.description: Response should mention the cookie recipe

scenarios.responseText:
Consider it preserved for posterity, sir! Agent has saved **Grandma's Chocolate Chip Cookies** as a note in the vault, complete with ingredients, baking instructions (375°F for 10 minutes), and a yield of 24 cookies. It's tagged with `recipe`, `cookies`, `baking`, `dessert`, and `grandma` for easy retrieval.

Should you wish, Agent could also create a dedicated **Recipe** content type to better organise any future culinary additions — handy if Grandma has more tricks up her sleeve.

scenarios.scenarioName: Pet owner adds two dogs and a cat

assertionResults.description: Response should mention Bailey
Response should mention Rigby
Response should mention Sgt Pepper

scenarios.responseText:
Noted with great fondness, sir! Agent has created a **My Pets** note in the vault recording your delightful household companions:

- 🐶 **Bailey** — dog
- 🐶 **Rigby** — dog
- 🐱 **Sgt Pepper** — cat

Agent will remember them well. Should you ever wish to log vet appointments, feeding schedules, or other pet-related matters, Agent can link those directly to this record.

scenarios.scenarioName: Book club member tracks books to read

assertionResults.description: Response should mention The Great Gatsby

scenarios.responseText:
Consider it done, sir. Agent has added **Read The Great Gatsby by F. Scott Fitzgerald** to your reading list as a task, due **Sunday, May 31**. It's been tagged `reading-list` and set to medium priority.

Should you wish, Agent could also set a mid-month check-in reminder around May 15 to keep you on track — Fitzgerald rewards a leisurely pace, but deadlines do have a way of sneaking up on one.

scenarios.scenarioName: Gardener tracks a new plant

assertionResults.description: Response should mention the tomato plant

scenarios.responseText:
Splendid news on the gardening front, boss! Agent has attended to the following:

- **Note created:** *Cherry Tomato* — logging the April 1st planting date, full sun requirement, and every-other-day watering schedule, tagged `garden`, `tomato`, `raised-bed`
- **Recurring task created:** *Water Cherry Tomato* — set to trigger daily (with a note inside reminding you the actual cadence is every other day)

One small matter to flag honestly: the link between the watering task and the Cherry Tomato note encountered a hiccup — the target recurrence couldn't be located to complete the connection. The entities themselves are safely saved, just not formally linked at this time. Agent can retry that link on your next interaction if you'd like.

Shall Agent also set a reminder for when to expect the first harvest, or perhaps track any companion plants nearby?

scenarios.scenarioName: Daily briefing skill returns task and event summary

assertionResults.description: Briefing should mention the high-priority budget task
Briefing should mention the standup event

scenarios.responseText:
Good morning! Here is your briefing for Wednesday, April 22:

- 🔴 **Review budget** — high-priority task awaiting your attention. Agent would suggest tackling this first whilst the mind is fresh.
- 📞 **Call client** — open task, medium priority. Worth scheduling a specific time today if possible.
- 📅 **Team standup** — noted in the vault from yesterday (April 21 at 9 AM). No events are on the books for today itself.

The calendar is quiet today, which gives you a fine window to knock out that budget review. Shall Agent set a due time for either task, or is there anything else you'd like to prepare for the day?

scenarios.scenarioName: Task with explicit priority sets the field

assertionResults.description: A task should be created for the budget review
Priority should be set to high as explicitly stated

scenarios.responseText:
Consider it done, sir. Agent has created **Review the Q2 budget** as a high-priority task, open and awaiting your attention.

Should you wish, Agent can link this task to a relevant goal or project — or set a due date to keep things moving along smartly.

scenarios.scenarioName: Project kickoff creates event + goal + task

assertionResults.description: A kickoff meeting event should be created
A website redesign goal should be created
assertionResults.message: Created goal: "Launch Website Redesign by June"

assertionResults.description: A task to send the brief should be created
assertionResults.actual: Send project brief to the team
assertionResults.message: Created task: "Send project brief to the team"

scenarios.responseText:
The website redesign project is now underway, boss. Agent has set up three items and linked them together:

- 📅 **Website Redesign Kickoff Meeting** — scheduled for Monday, April 27 at 9:00 AM
- 🎯 **Launch Website Redesign by June** — goal set with a target date of June 30
- ✅ **Send project brief to the team** — task due Monday, April 27, linked to the goal

The task and meeting are both tied to the launch goal to keep everything connected in the knowledge graph.

Might Agent suggest also creating a **Website Redesign** project note to serve as a central hub, or adding the team members as people in the vault so tasks can be assigned to them?

scenarios.scenarioName: Mark an existing task done (update, not create)

assertionResults.message: task "Send quarterly report" was updated (timestamp changed)

assertionResults.message: No unwanted task matching "quarterly report" was created

scenarios.responseText:
Splendid work, sir! **Send quarterly report** has been marked complete. A high-priority task, now satisfyingly put to rest.

Is there anything else requiring attention — perhaps a follow-up note or a new task to log?

scenarios.scenarioName: Meeting notes create a note, not a task

assertionResults.description: A note should be created for meeting notes
Should not create a task for a note

scenarios.responseText:
Noted, sir. Agent has created **Migration Timeline Discussion** — capturing that engineering has targeted end of May for the migration completion.

scenarios.scenarioName: Add a client contact with role and email

assertionResults.description: A person entity should be created for Marcus
Email should be set correctly

scenarios.responseText:
Consider it done, sir. Agent has added **Marcus Williams** to your contacts — VP of Engineering at CloudBase, reachable at marcus@cloudbase.io.

A company entity for **CloudBase** was also created and linked to Marcus. Should you wish to add any colleagues from CloudBase in future, they can be connected to the same organisation.

Might Agent also log any notes about your relationship with Marcus, or schedule a follow-up?

scenarios.scenarioName: Stop-doing creates todont without errors

assertionResults.description: A todont should be created
assertionResults.message: Created todont: "Eat fast food for lunch"

assertionResults.description: Should not create a task for a stop-doing
assertionResults.message: No unwanted task matching "fast food" was created

scenarios.responseText:
Noted and committed to memory, sir. Agent has created a **To-Don't: Eat fast food for lunch**, logged as a deliberate health commitment. It now sits in the vault as a standing intention — a quiet reminder of what you've decided you're better off without.

Should you wish, Agent could also create a complementary goal around healthier lunch habits, or link this to an existing health goal if one exists. Just say the word.

scenarios.scenarioName: Goal creation succeeds cleanly

assertionResults.description: A goal should be created for reading books

scenarios.responseText:
Splendid ambition, sir! Agent has set everything in motion:

- 🎯 **Goal created:** *Read 24 Books This Year* — priority high, target date December 31, 2026
- ✅ **Task created:** *Read 2 books this month* — linked to the goal as a supporting task

The task is connected to your goal, so progress here feeds directly into the bigger picture.

A couple of follow-ups worth considering:
- Shall Agent create a **book tracking content type** so you can log each title as you finish it?
- Would you like a **recurring monthly task** set up automatically — so "Read 2 books this month" renews each month through December?
